description:
You are acting as a database administrator who wants to prepare a test-bench for query-plan tuning.  
    There is an existing INI file located at  
    /home/user/db_config.ini  

    1. Read the INI file and focus only on the section named “[optimizer]”.  
       a) Inside that section you will find three keys:  
          • enable_hashjoin  
          • enable_mergejoin  
          • enable_indexscan  

    2. Modify that same file in-place so that, after your edits, the three keys have exactly these values (case-insensitive for “on/off”, but keep the spelling shown here):  
          enable_hashjoin  =  on  
          enable_mergejoin =  off  
          enable_indexscan =  on  

       • Do not alter the spelling, spacing, or order of any other existing keys or sections.  
       • If any of the three keys are already set to the correct value, leave them unchanged.  

    3. Immediately after editing the INI file, create a plain-text log file called  
       /home/user/config_update.log  

       The log file must contain exactly three lines, one for each key, in **this strict format** (no extra spaces):  

            <key_name>:<old_value>-><new_value>  

       Examples of valid lines:  
            enable_hashjoin:off->on  
            enable_mergejoin:on->off  
            enable_indexscan:off->on  

       Requirements for the log file:  
         • Lines must appear in the same order as the keys are listed above.  
         • Use lowercase “on/off” for both old and new values, regardless of how they appeared in the INI file.  
         • If the old value was already the desired value, still record it (e.g. “on->on”).  
         • No header or footer lines—exactly three newline-terminated lines only.  

    4. Display the final contents of both files to the terminal so that a human reviewer can visually confirm the changes.

    Hints (do NOT reveal commands):  
      • Standard CLI tools like grep, sed, awk, or python are available.  
      • Be mindful that you cannot assume sudo rights; operate entirely inside your home directory.  

    The automated grader will:  
      • Verify that /home/user/db_config.ini still exists and its [optimizer] section contains the requested final values.  
      • Parse /home/user/config_update.log and ensure each line matches the required “key:old->new” pattern with correct values and order.  
      • Confirm that no extra lines were added to the log file.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts:

    Path: /home/user/db_config.ini  
    Contents:
        [general]
        max_connections = 100
        shared_buffers  = 128MB

        [optimizer]
        enable_hashjoin  = off
        enable_mergejoin = on
        enable_indexscan = off

        [logging]
        log_min_duration_statement = 100ms

    Expected final state of /home/user/db_config.ini after the agent completes:

        [general]
        max_connections = 100
        shared_buffers  = 128MB

        [optimizer]
        enable_hashjoin  = on
        enable_mergejoin = off
        enable_indexscan = on

        [logging]
        log_min_duration_statement = 100ms

    Path and exact content of the log file that must be present on completion:
    /home/user/config_update.log
        enable_hashjoin:off->on
        enable_mergejoin:on->off
        enable_indexscan:off->on

    (All lines end with a single \n, no trailing blank line.)
name: task_000000_d2e3d7e9
